title: Seriphidium sogdianum
taxonomy: Seriphidium sogdianum is classified as a species within the genus Seriphidium, which is a part of the family Asteraceae. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the order Asterales, which comprises other flowering plants with similar characteristics. The class of this plant is Magnoliopsida, which includes flowering plants with a specific arrangement of sepals and petals. intro: Seriphidium sogdianum, also known as Sogdian wormwood, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Seriphidium sogdianum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Seriphidium sogdianum is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and fever.
